problem.title: Shortest Palindrome
problem.description: You are given a string s. You can convert s to a palindrome by adding characters in front of it.
assumptions: Add characters only to the front.

example.explanation: Find longest palindromic prefix, prepend reverse of remaining suffix. Use KMP to find overlap.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – KMP Algorithm [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Concatenate s, a separator '#', and the reverse of s to form a new string, then compute the KMP failure function on it. The last value of the failure table tells us the length of the longest palindromic prefix of s, so we only need to prepend the remaining suffix (reversed) to make s a palindrome. This avoids O(n²) brute-force checking by reusing the efficient prefix-matching logic of KMP.
